definitions.zh: 委派与移交是以委派过程为关键概念的本体边界；DelegationProcess、HandoffProcess、委派阶段、委派事件、委派结果、契约与约定。 其成员归属遵循：当对象的身份由一次工作者选择、委派契约、发起接受撤销完成阶段，或控制和责任的转移决定时，在此拥有。
definitions.en: Delegation and handoff is the ontology boundary anchored by the key notion delegation process; DelegationProcess, HandoffProcess, delegation phases, events, outcomes, contracts, and agreements. Membership follows: Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.
definitions.ja: 委任と引き継ぎは委任プロセスを主要概念とするオントロジー境界です。DelegationProcess、HandoffProcess、委任段階、イベント、結果、契約、合意。 所属判定は次に従います：対象の同一性が一回の作業者選択、委任契約、開始・受諾・取消・完了段階、または制御・責任移転で決まる場合に所有します。

purpose.zh: 管理工作者选择、委派约定及控制或责任移交的完整过程，使发起、接受、撤销和完成均可追踪。
purpose.en: Manage worker selection, delegation agreements, and transfer of control or responsibility as complete processes whose initiation, acceptance, revocation, and completion are traceable.
purpose.ja: 作業者選択、委任合意、制御・責任の引き継ぎを完全な過程として管理し、開始、受諾、取消、完了を追跡可能にします。

includes.zh: DelegationProcess、HandoffProcess、委派阶段、委派事件、委派结果、契约与约定。
includes.en: DelegationProcess, HandoffProcess, delegation phases, events, outcomes, contracts, and agreements.
includes.ja: DelegationProcess、HandoffProcess、委任段階、イベント、結果、契約、合意。

includes.zh: WorkerSelection、WorkerPool、WorkerRole、可用性评估、能力匹配、选择决定和工作分配阶段。
includes.en: WorkerSelection, WorkerPool, WorkerRole, availability assessment, capability matching, selection-decision, and worker-assignment phases.
includes.ja: WorkerSelection、WorkerPool、WorkerRole、可用性評価、能力照合、選択判断、作業割当段階。

excludes.zh: 协作角色类型与长期 ActorRoleBinding 归协同角色；委派只引用发起者和接受者当时承担的角色。
excludes.en: Coordination-role types and longer-lived ActorRoleBinding values belong to coordination roles; delegation only references roles borne by initiator and recipient at that time.
excludes.ja: coordination-role type と longer-lived ActorRoleBinding は coordination role に属し、delegation は initiator/recipient が当時担う role だけを参照します。

excludes.zh: 工具 Invocation 由能力调用语义决定；把 Agent 当作 Tool 调用不自动等于 Delegation。
excludes.en: Tool Invocation is determined by capability-call semantics; invoking an agent as a Tool is not automatically Delegation.
excludes.ja: ツール Invocation は能力呼出し意味で決まり、Agent を Tool として呼び出しても自動的に Delegation にはなりません。

description.zh: 委派与移交的输入概念族为委派契约、委派上下文隔离规范、委派上下文、工作者候选池、工作项；责任交接由已接受事实 委派过程 —governed_by→ 委派契约 (DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract)；委派契约 —specifies→ 委派上下文隔离规范 (DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation)；委派上下文隔离规范 —bounds→ 委派上下文 (ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext)；委派契约 —specifies→ 工作项 (DelegationContract-specifies-WorkItem)；工作者选择过程 —uses→ 工作者候选池 (WorkerSelectionProcess-uses-WorkerPool)；任务分发 —assigns→ 工作项 (TaskDistribution-assigns-WorkItem) 明确给出。
description.en: The input families for Delegation and handoff are delegation contract, Delegation context-isolation specification, Delegation context, worker pool, work item; the accepted facts delegation process —governed_by→ delegation contract (DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract); delegation contract —specifies→ Delegation context-isolation specification (DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation); Delegation context-isolation specification —bounds→ Delegation context (ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext); delegation contract —specifies→ work item (DelegationContract-specifies-WorkItem); Worker selection process —uses→ worker pool (WorkerSelectionProcess-uses-WorkerPool); task distribution —assigns→ work item (TaskDistribution-assigns-WorkItem) make the responsibility handoff explicit.
description.ja: 委任と引き継ぎの入力概念族は委任契約、委任コンテキスト分離仕様、委任コンテキスト、ワーカー候補プール、作業項目です。責任の受け渡しは承認済み事実 委任プロセス —governed_by→ 委任契約 (DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract)；委任契約 —specifies→ 委任コンテキスト分離仕様 (DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation)；委任コンテキスト分離仕様 —bounds→ 委任コンテキスト (ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext)；委任契約 —specifies→ 作業項目 (DelegationContract-specifies-WorkItem)；ワーカー選択プロセス —uses→ ワーカー候補プール (WorkerSelectionProcess-uses-WorkerPool)；タスク分配 —assigns→ 作業項目 (TaskDistribution-assigns-WorkItem) により明示されます。

description.zh: 委派与移交的输出概念族为委派结果、工作者可用性、工作者能力匹配证据、工作者选择、控制移交、责任转移；责任交接由已接受事实 委派过程 —produces→ 委派结果 (DelegationProcess-produces-DelegationResult)；工作者可用性评估 —produces→ 工作者可用性 (WorkerAvailabilityAssessment-produces-WorkerAvailability)；工作者能力匹配 —produces→ 工作者能力匹配证据 (WorkerCapabilityMatching-produces-WorkerCapabilityMatch)；工作者选择决定活动 —produces→ 工作者选择 (WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity-produces-WorkerSelection)；移交过程 —produces→ 控制移交 (HandoffProcess-produces-Handoff)；移交过程 —results_in→ 责任转移 (HandoffProcess-results_in-ResponsibilityTransfer) 明确给出。
description.en: The output families for Delegation and handoff are delegation result, worker availability, worker capability-match evidence, worker selection, handoff, responsibility transfer; the accepted facts delegation process —produces→ delegation result (DelegationProcess-produces-DelegationResult); Worker availability assessment —produces→ worker availability (WorkerAvailabilityAssessment-produces-WorkerAvailability); Worker capability matching —produces→ worker capability-match evidence (WorkerCapabilityMatching-produces-WorkerCapabilityMatch); Worker selection decision activity —produces→ worker selection (WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity-produces-WorkerSelection); Handoff process —produces→ handoff (HandoffProcess-produces-Handoff); Handoff process —results_in→ responsibility transfer (HandoffProcess-results_in-ResponsibilityTransfer) make the responsibility handoff explicit.
description.ja: 委任と引き継ぎの出力概念族は委任結果、作業者可用性、作業者能力一致証拠、作業者選択、ハンドオフ、責任移転です。責任の受け渡しは承認済み事実 委任プロセス —produces→ 委任結果 (DelegationProcess-produces-DelegationResult)；ワーカー可用性評価 —produces→ 作業者可用性 (WorkerAvailabilityAssessment-produces-WorkerAvailability)；ワーカー能力照合 —produces→ 作業者能力一致証拠 (WorkerCapabilityMatching-produces-WorkerCapabilityMatch)；ワーカー選択判断活動 —produces→ 作業者選択 (WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity-produces-WorkerSelection)；引き継ぎプロセス —produces→ ハンドオフ (HandoffProcess-produces-Handoff)；引き継ぎプロセス —results_in→ 責任移転 (HandoffProcess-results_in-ResponsibilityTransfer) により明示されます。

not_applicable_reason.zh: 该本体不负责判定运行失败。计划、运行、角色或安全范围需要说明一项工作如何被交给另一主体时，引用委派过程和当前契约状态。
not_applicable_reason.en: This ontology does not own runtime-failure adjudication. Planning, runtime, role, or safety scopes reference the delegation process and current contract state when explaining how work was assigned to another actor.
not_applicable_reason.ja: このオントロジーは実行失敗の判定を所有しません。計画、実行、役割、安全範囲が作業を別主体へ渡した経緯を示すとき、委任過程と現在契約状態を参照します。

not_applicable_reason.zh: 该本体不负责启动恢复流程。计划、运行、角色或安全范围需要说明一项工作如何被交给另一主体时，引用委派过程和当前契约状态。
not_applicable_reason.en: This ontology does not initiate a recovery workflow. Planning, runtime, role, or safety scopes reference the delegation process and current contract state when explaining how work was assigned to another actor.
not_applicable_reason.ja: このオントロジーは回復ワークフローを開始しません。計画、実行、役割、安全範囲が作業を別主体へ渡した経緯を示すとき、委任過程と現在契約状態を参照します。

decision_note.zh: 委派与移交与协同角色按唯一 owner 和可判定问题分界。
decision_note.en: Delegation and handoff and Coordination roles are separated by unique ownership and answerable questions.
decision_note.ja: 委任と引き継ぎと協調ロールは一意の所有者と判定可能な問いで分離されます。

decision_note.zh: 委派与移交与协同角色按唯一 owner 和可判定问题分界。
decision_note.en: Delegation and handoff and Coordination roles are separated by unique ownership and answerable questions.

decision_note.ja: 委任と引き継ぎと協調ロールは一意の所有者と判定可能な問いで分離されます。

labels.zh: 某次委派当前处于发起、接受、撤销还是完成阶段，哪条事件使状态生效？：正例
labels.en: Is a delegation currently initiated, accepted, revoked, or completed, and which event made that state effective?: positive case
labels.ja: ある委任は現在、開始、受諾、取消、完了のどの段階で、どのイベントが状態を有効にしましたか。：正例

descriptions.zh: 该问的正例同时触及 owner 与相关本体，并保持真实关系方向：任务分发 —assigns_work_item_to→ 工作者角色 (assigns_work_item_to)。
descriptions.en: The positive case touches both the owner and related ontology while preserving the actual relation direction: task distribution —assigns_work_item_to→ Worker role (assigns_work_item_to).
descriptions.ja: 正例は owner と関連オントロジーの双方に触れ、実在する関係方向を保持します：タスク分配 —assigns_work_item_to→ ワーカーロール (assigns_work_item_to)。

expected_result.zh: 查询应满足：Exactly one current state follows a valid transition path, with the causative event and actor recorded.
expected_result.en: The query must satisfy: Exactly one current state follows a valid transition path, with the causative event and actor recorded.
expected_result.ja: 照会は次を満たす必要があります：Exactly one current state follows a valid transition path, with the causative event and actor recorded.

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 正例使用逐问独立的证据 ID，并保留 owner、相关本体和关系适用性的显式记录。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The positive case has question-specific evidence IDs and explicitly records the owner, related ontology, and relation applicability.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 正例は質問固有の証拠 ID を持ち、owner、関連オントロジー、関係適用性を明示的に記録します。

source_claims.supports: Source eng-fw-crewai-docs at Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes supports operational notions used by Moonweave CQ orchestration-delegation-handoff-cq-process-state-v3 (orchestration-delegation-handoff.process-state), owned by orchestration-delegation-handoff and compared with orchestration-actors-delegation. The source does not define Moonweave CQ orchestration-delegation-handoff-cq-process-state-v3, its query, assertion, owner, or evidence binding. The query "Order DelegationEvent values by process sequence, validate allowed phase transitions, and return the latest effective non-superseded state." and expected assertion "Exactly one current state follows a valid transition path, with the causative event and actor recorded." are a Moonweave design inference over the registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes

The source does not define Moonweave CQ orchestration-delegation-handoff-cq-process-state-v3, its query, assertion, owner, or evidence binding. The query "Order DelegationEvent values by process sequence, validate allowed phase transitions, and return the latest effective non-superseded state." and expected assertion "Exactly one current state follows a valid transition path, with the causative event and actor recorded." are a Moonweave design inference over the registered evidence.

labels.zh: 某次委派当前处于发起、接受、撤销还是完成阶段，哪条事件使状态生效？：反例
labels.en: Is a delegation currently initiated, accepted, revoked, or completed, and which event made that state effective?: counterexample
labels.ja: ある委任は現在、開始、受諾、取消、完了のどの段階で、どのイベントが状態を有効にしましたか。：反例

descriptions.zh: 反例仍列出同一 owner 与相关本体端点，但拒绝仅凭名称相似、相邻出现或反向读取关系来满足“某次委派当前处于发起、接受、撤销还是完成阶段，哪条事件使状态生效？”。
descriptions.en: The counterexample still names the same owner and related-ontology endpoints, but rejects satisfying “Is a delegation currently initiated, accepted, revoked, or completed, and which event made that state effective?” through name similarity, adjacency, or a reversed relation.
descriptions.ja: 反例も同じ owner と関連オントロジー端点を示しますが、名称類似、隣接、関係の逆読みによって「ある委任は現在、開始、受諾、取消、完了のどの段階で、どのイベントが状態を有効にしましたか。」を満たすことを拒否します。

expected_result.zh: 查询不得返回越权 owner、颠倒关系方向或缺少显式 N/A 判定的候选。
expected_result.en: The query must reject a candidate with the wrong owner, reversed relation direction, or a missing explicit N/A decision.
expected_result.ja: 照会は、owner の越境、関係方向の逆転、または明示的 N/A 判断の欠落がある候補を拒否しなければなりません。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 反例与正例拥有不同 ID，并以同一组真实节点说明哪些推断不被关系和 owner 规则支持。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The counterexample has a distinct ID and uses the same real nodes to show which inference is unsupported by relation and ownership rules.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 反例は正例と異なる ID を持ち、同じ実在ノードにより関係規則と所有規則が支持しない推論を示します。

labels.zh: 候选工作者经过哪些可用性、能力和约束判断后被选择，落选原因是什么？：正例
labels.en: Through which availability, capability, and constraint assessments was a worker selected, and why were others rejected?: positive case
labels.ja: 候補作業者はどの可用性、能力、制約判断を経て選ばれ、他候補の不採用理由は何ですか。：正例

descriptions.zh: 该问的正例以 工作者选择、提示选择决策、工作者选择决定活动、选择决策 作边界比较；问题“候选工作者经过哪些可用性、能力和约束判断后被选择，落选原因是什么？”使用相关本体作边界对照，但 canonical 图中没有可支持该问法的已接受非分类跨模块关系；因此例证并列双方候选概念，不声称运行时传递。
descriptions.en: The positive case compares worker selection, prompt selection decision, Worker selection decision activity, Selection Decision at the ownership boundary. The question “Through which availability, capability, and constraint assessments was a worker selected, and why were others rejected?” uses the related ontology as a boundary comparison, but the canonical graph has no accepted non-taxonomic cross-module relation supporting that reading; the examples therefore juxtapose both candidate families without claiming a runtime handoff.
descriptions.ja: 正例は 作業者選択、プロンプト選択判断、ワーカー選択判断活動、選択決定 を所有境界で比較します。質問「候補作業者はどの可用性、能力、制約判断を経て選ばれ、他候補の不採用理由は何ですか。」は関連オントロジーを境界対照として使いますが、その読みに根拠を与える承認済みの非分類モジュール間関係は canonical グラフにありません。そのため例証は双方の候補概念を並置し、実行時の受け渡しを主張しません。

expected_result.zh: 查询应满足：The selected WorkerRole satisfies every mandatory condition; every rejected role has a stage-specific reason.
expected_result.en: The query must satisfy: The selected WorkerRole satisfies every mandatory condition; every rejected role has a stage-specific reason.
expected_result.ja: 照会は次を満たす必要があります：The selected WorkerRole satisfies every mandatory condition; every rejected role has a stage-specific reason.

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 正例使用逐问独立的证据 ID，并保留 owner、相关本体和关系适用性的显式记录。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The positive case has question-specific evidence IDs and explicitly records the owner, related ontology, and relation applicability.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 正例は質問固有の証拠 ID を持ち、owner、関連オントロジー、関係適用性を明示的に記録します。

source_claims.supports: Source eng-fw-crewai-docs at Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes supports operational notions used by Moonweave CQ orchestration-delegation-handoff-cq-worker-selection-v3 (orchestration-delegation-handoff.worker-selection), owned by orchestration-delegation-handoff and compared with tool-discovery-selection. The source does not define Moonweave CQ orchestration-delegation-handoff-cq-worker-selection-v3, its query, assertion, owner, or evidence binding. The query "Trace WorkerSelectionProcess from WorkerPool through WorkerAvailabilityAssessment, WorkerCapabilityMatching, WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity, and WorkerAssignment." and expected assertion "The selected WorkerRole satisfies every mandatory condition; every rejected role has a stage-specific reason." are a Moonweave design inference over the registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes

source_claims.locator: Overview > Durable execution, human-in-the-loop, memory, and subgraphs

labels.zh: 移交前后谁拥有任务控制权和结果责任，未被移交的权限或预算有哪些？：正例
labels.en: Who holds task control and outcome responsibility before and after handoff, and which permissions or budgets were not transferred?: positive case
labels.ja: handoff 前後で誰が task control/outcome responsibility を持ち、transfer されない permission/budget は何ですか。：正例

descriptions.zh: 该问的正例同时触及 owner 与相关本体，并保持真实关系方向：任务分发 —distributes_task_to→ 智能体参与者 (distributes_task_to)。
descriptions.en: The positive case touches both the owner and related ontology while preserving the actual relation direction: task distribution —distributes_task_to→ agent actor (distributes_task_to).
descriptions.ja: 正例は owner と関連オントロジーの双方に触れ、実在する関係方向を保持します：タスク分配 —distributes_task_to→ エージェントアクター (distributes_task_to)。

expected_result.zh: 查询应满足：Transferred and retained responsibilities are disjoint and complete for the contract scope; no permission is inferred from task transfer.
expected_result.en: The query must satisfy: Transferred and retained responsibilities are disjoint and complete for the contract scope; no permission is inferred from task transfer.
expected_result.ja: 照会は次を満たす必要があります：Transferred and retained responsibilities are disjoint and complete for the contract scope; no permission is inferred from task transfer.

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 正例使用逐问独立的证据 ID，并保留 owner、相关本体和关系适用性的显式记录。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The positive case has question-specific evidence IDs and explicitly records the owner, related ontology, and relation applicability.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 正例は質問固有の証拠 ID を持ち、owner、関連オントロジー、関係適用性を明示的に記録します。

labels.zh: 移交前后谁拥有任务控制权和结果责任，未被移交的权限或预算有哪些？：反例
labels.en: Who holds task control and outcome responsibility before and after handoff, and which permissions or budgets were not transferred?: counterexample
labels.ja: handoff 前後で誰が task control/outcome responsibility を持ち、transfer されない permission/budget は何ですか。：反例

descriptions.zh: 反例仍列出同一 owner 与相关本体端点，但拒绝仅凭名称相似、相邻出现或反向读取关系来满足“移交前后谁拥有任务控制权和结果责任，未被移交的权限或预算有哪些？”。
descriptions.en: The counterexample still names the same owner and related-ontology endpoints, but rejects satisfying “Who holds task control and outcome responsibility before and after handoff, and which permissions or budgets were not transferred?” through name similarity, adjacency, or a reversed relation.
descriptions.ja: 反例も同じ owner と関連オントロジー端点を示しますが、名称類似、隣接、関係の逆読みによって「handoff 前後で誰が task control/outcome responsibility を持ち、transfer されない permission/budget は何ですか。」を満たすことを拒否します。

expected_result.zh: 查询不得返回越权 owner、颠倒关系方向或缺少显式 N/A 判定的候选。
expected_result.en: The query must reject a candidate with the wrong owner, reversed relation direction, or a missing explicit N/A decision.
expected_result.ja: 照会は、owner の越境、関係方向の逆転、または明示的 N/A 判断の欠落がある候補を拒否しなければなりません。

competency_questions.query: Trace WorkerSelectionProcess from WorkerPool through WorkerAvailabilityAssessment, WorkerCapabilityMatching, WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity, and WorkerAssignment.
competency_questions.expected_assertion: The selected WorkerRole satisfies every mandatory condition; every rejected role has a stage-specific reason.

not_applicable_reason.zh: 问题“候选工作者经过哪些可用性、能力和约束判断后被选择，落选原因是什么？”使用相关本体作边界对照，但 canonical 图中没有可支持该问法的已接受非分类跨模块关系；因此例证并列双方候选概念，不声称运行时传递。
not_applicable_reason.en: The question “Through which availability, capability, and constraint assessments was a worker selected, and why were others rejected?” uses the related ontology as a boundary comparison, but the canonical graph has no accepted non-taxonomic cross-module relation supporting that reading; the examples therefore juxtapose both candidate families without claiming a runtime handoff.
not_applicable_reason.ja: 質問「候補作業者はどの可用性、能力、制約判断を経て選ばれ、他候補の不採用理由は何ですか。」は関連オントロジーを境界対照として使いますが、その読みに根拠を与える承認済みの非分類モジュール間関係は canonical グラフにありません。そのため例証は双方の候補概念を並置し、実行時の受け渡しを主張しません。

decision_note.ja: 本体審査は orchestration-delegation-handoff の実際の主要概念、重複相手、相互作用方向、明示ルート・バックボーン数を項目別に確認しました：key_notion=DelegationProcess; overlap_modules=[orchestration-actors-delegation,tool-invocation-execution,tool-discovery-selection,runtime-actors]; interaction=DelegationContract|ContextIsolation|DelegationContext|WorkerPool|WorkItem -> DelegationResult|WorkerAvailability|WorkerCapabilityMatch|WorkerSelection|Handoff|ResponsibilityTransfer; roots=1; backbone=28。本体審査はドメイン質問の所有を決定しません。

decision_note.zh: orchestration-delegation-handoff 的三语名称按实际值逐项复核：zh="委派与移交"; en="Delegation and handoff"; ja="委任と引き継ぎ"；purpose、边界和 CQ 表达也已复核，语言审查不决定 owner 或 backbone。
decision_note.en: orchestration-delegation-handoff's actual trilingual labels were checked exactly: zh="委派与移交"; en="Delegation and handoff"; ja="委任と引き継ぎ"; purpose, boundary, and CQ wording were also reviewed, while language review does not decide owner or backbone.
decision_note.ja: orchestration-delegation-handoff の実際の三言語ラベルを値どおりに確認しました：zh="委派与移交"; en="Delegation and handoff"; ja="委任と引き継ぎ"。purpose、境界、CQ 表現も審査し、言語審査は owner や backbone を決定しません。

change_note.zh: 委派与移交在 v3 中完成命名、owner、交互合同、CQ 与任意深度骨架复审。
change_note.en: Delegation and handoff completed its v3 naming, ownership, interaction-contract, CQ, and arbitrary-depth-backbone review.
change_note.ja: 委任と引き継ぎは v3 で名称、所有権、相互作用契約、CQ、任意深度バックボーンの再審査を完了しました。

owns_when.zh: 当对象的身份由一次工作者选择、委派契约、发起接受撤销完成阶段，或控制和责任的转移决定时，在此拥有。
owns_when.en: Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.
owns_when.ja: 対象の同一性が一回の作業者選択、委任契約、開始・受諾・取消・完了段階、または制御・責任移転で決まる場合に所有します。

references_when.zh: 计划、运行、角色或安全范围需要说明一项工作如何被交给另一主体时，引用委派过程和当前契约状态。
references_when.en: Planning, runtime, role, or safety scopes reference the delegation process and current contract state when explaining how work was assigned to another actor.
references_when.ja: 計画、実行、役割、安全範囲が作業を別主体へ渡した経緯を示すとき、委任過程と現在契約状態を参照します。

rationale.zh: 委派与移交拥有选择、契约、阶段、事件和责任转移；协同角色拥有可复用职责类型与角色绑定。 本条仅记录 CoordinationOwnershipRecord、SubagentRole、WorkerRole；其 canonical owner 是协同角色（orchestration-actors-delegation），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此引用这些概念。
rationale.en: Delegation and handoff own selection, contracts, phases, events, and responsibility transfer; coordination roles own reusable responsibility types and role bindings. This record is limited to CoordinationOwnershipRecord, SubagentRole, WorkerRole; their canonical owner is Coordination roles (orchestration-actors-delegation), so orchestration-delegation-handoff references these concepts here.
rationale.ja: 委任と引き継ぎは選択、契約、段階、イベント、責任移転を所有し、協調ロールは再利用可能な責務型と役割バインディングを所有します。 この記録は CoordinationOwnershipRecord、SubagentRole、WorkerRole だけを対象とし、canonical owner は協調ロール（orchestration-actors-delegation）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを参照します。

source_claims.supports: Source eng-fw-crewai-docs at Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes supports operational notions used to compare orchestration-delegation-handoff with orchestration-actors-delegation, including CoordinationOwnershipRecord, SubagentRole, WorkerRole. The source does not define Moonweave Modules orchestration-delegation-handoff or orchestration-actors-delegation, their class or relation placement, or their ownership split. Under orchestration-delegation-handoff's owns_when "Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.", Moonweave applies the disambiguation test "If role, scope, and validity interval suffice for identity, it belongs to coordination roles; if delegation ID, contract, and phase event are required, it belongs to delegation and handoff."; result=references and owner=orchestration-actors-delegation are a Moonweave design inference over the registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation > Agents, tasks, crews, flows, and processes

rationale.zh: 委派与移交拥有转交有范围工作、权限、上下文或控制责任的过程与契约；调用与执行拥有管理者保留控制和答案责任的 AgentAsToolInvocation。 本条仅记录 DelegationContract、Handoff；其 canonical owner 是委派与移交（orchestration-delegation-handoff），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此拥有这些概念。
rationale.en: Delegation and handoff own processes and contracts transferring scoped work, authority, context, or control responsibility; invocation and execution own AgentAsToolInvocation when the manager retains control and answer responsibility. This record is limited to DelegationContract, Handoff; their canonical owner is Delegation and handoff (orchestration-delegation-handoff), so orchestration-delegation-handoff owns these concepts here.
rationale.ja: 委任と引き継ぎは範囲付き作業・権限・コンテキスト・制御責任を移すプロセスと契約を所有し、呼び出しと実行は管理者が制御と回答責任を保持する AgentAsToolInvocation を所有します。 この記録は DelegationContract、Handoff だけを対象とし、canonical owner は委任と引き継ぎ（orchestration-delegation-handoff）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを所有します。

relation_not_applicable_reason.zh: “委派与移交”与“调用与执行”之间没有适用于本 owner 概念组的已接受跨模块事实；该决策只比较明确列出的候选概念，不暗示运行时交互。
relation_not_applicable_reason.en: There is no accepted cross-module fact applicable to this owner-homogeneous concept group between Delegation and handoff and Invocation and execution; the decision compares only the named candidates without implying runtime interaction.
relation_not_applicable_reason.ja: 「委任と引き継ぎ」と「呼び出しと実行」の間には、この owner 単一概念群に適用できる承認済みモジュール間事実がありません。明示候補だけを比較し、実行時相互作用を示唆しません。

Under orchestration-delegation-handoff's owns_when "Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.", Moonweave applies the disambiguation test "If a delegation contract transfers work scope, authority, or responsibility, it belongs to delegation and handoff; if the caller retains final-answer and conversation control and receives a ToolResult, it belongs to invocation and execution."; result=references and owner=tool-invocation-execution are a Moonweave design inference over the registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Overview > Durable execution, human-in-the-loop, memory, and subgraphs

decision_note.zh: 委派与移交拥有转交有范围工作、权限、上下文或控制责任的过程与契约；调用与执行拥有管理者保留控制和答案责任的 AgentAsToolInvocation。 本条仅记录 AgentAsToolInvocation、ProgrammaticToolCall；其 canonical owner 是调用与执行（tool-invocation-execution），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此引用这些概念。
decision_note.en: Delegation and handoff own processes and contracts transferring scoped work, authority, context, or control responsibility; invocation and execution own AgentAsToolInvocation when the manager retains control and answer responsibility. This record is limited to AgentAsToolInvocation, ProgrammaticToolCall; their canonical owner is Invocation and execution (tool-invocation-execution), so orchestration-delegation-handoff references these concepts here.
decision_note.ja: 委任と引き継ぎは範囲付き作業・権限・コンテキスト・制御責任を移すプロセスと契約を所有し、呼び出しと実行は管理者が制御と回答責任を保持する AgentAsToolInvocation を所有します。 この記録は AgentAsToolInvocation、ProgrammaticToolCall だけを対象とし、canonical owner は呼び出しと実行（tool-invocation-execution）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを参照します。

decision_note.zh: 委派与移交拥有转交有范围工作、权限、上下文或控制责任的过程与契约；调用与执行拥有管理者保留控制和答案责任的 AgentAsToolInvocation。 本条仅记录 AgentAsToolInvocation、ProgrammaticToolCall；其 canonical owner 是调用与执行（tool-invocation-execution），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此引用这些概念。

rationale.zh: 委派与移交拥有 WorkerPool、availability assessment、capability matching、WorkerSelectionProcess、DelegationContract 和责任转移；能力发现拥有工具候选、匹配与 ToolSelectionDecision。 本条仅记录 WorkerSelection、WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity；其 canonical owner 是委派与移交（orchestration-delegation-handoff），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此拥有这些概念。
rationale.en: Delegation and handoff own WorkerPool, availability assessment, capability matching, WorkerSelectionProcess, DelegationContract, and responsibility transfer; capability discovery owns tool candidates, matching, and ToolSelectionDecision. This record is limited to WorkerSelection, WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity; their canonical owner is Delegation and handoff (orchestration-delegation-handoff), so orchestration-delegation-handoff owns these concepts here.
rationale.ja: delegation/handoff は WorkerPool、availability assessment、capability matching、WorkerSelectionProcess、DelegationContract、responsibility transfer を所有し、capability discovery は tool candidate、matching、ToolSelectionDecision を所有します。 この記録は WorkerSelection、WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity だけを対象とし、canonical owner は委任と引き継ぎ（orchestration-delegation-handoff）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを所有します。

relation_not_applicable_reason.zh: “委派与移交”与“能力发现与选择”之间没有适用于本 owner 概念组的已接受跨模块事实；该决策只比较明确列出的候选概念，不暗示运行时交互。
relation_not_applicable_reason.en: There is no accepted cross-module fact applicable to this owner-homogeneous concept group between Delegation and handoff and Capability discovery and selection; the decision compares only the named candidates without implying runtime interaction.
relation_not_applicable_reason.ja: 「委任と引き継ぎ」と「能力発見と選択」の間には、この owner 単一概念群に適用できる承認済みモジュール間事実がありません。明示候補だけを比較し、実行時相互作用を示唆しません。

decision_note.ja: delegation/handoff は一 collaboration の candidate assessment、selection phase、contract、budget、context bound、handoff event を所有し、runtime actor は selected Actor の stable identity/capability binding を所有します。 この記録は AgentActor だけを対象とし、canonical owner は実行参加者とバインディング（runtime-actors）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを参照します。

semantic_area.zh: 委派接受者与协作角色承担者
semantic_area.ja: 委任受任者と協調役割担当者

overlap_reason.zh: 同一 AgentActor 可承担 WorkerRole 并成为 Handoff 的控制接收方，但前者是可复用职责绑定，后者是一次移交关系中的运行主体端点。 本条仅记录 Orchestrator、WorkerRole；其 canonical owner 是协同角色（orchestration-actors-delegation），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此引用这些概念。
overlap_reason.en: The same AgentActor may bear WorkerRole and receive control through Handoff, but the former is a reusable responsibility binding while the latter is a runtime-actor endpoint of one transfer relation. This record is limited to Orchestrator, WorkerRole; their canonical owner is Coordination roles (orchestration-actors-delegation), so orchestration-delegation-handoff references these concepts here.
overlap_reason.ja: 同じ AgentActor は WorkerRole を担い Handoff で制御を受領できますが、前者は再利用可能な責務バインディング、後者は一回の移転関係における実行アクター端点です。 この記録は Orchestrator、WorkerRole だけを対象とし、canonical owner は協調ロール（orchestration-actors-delegation）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを参照します。

disambiguation_test.zh: 若只需角色、作用域和有效期即可识别，则归协同角色；若必须有 delegation id、契约和阶段事件，则归委派与移交。
disambiguation_test.en: If role, scope, and validity interval suffice for identity, it belongs to coordination roles; if delegation ID, contract, and phase event are required, it belongs to delegation and handoff.
disambiguation_test.ja: 役割、作用域、有効期間だけで識別できれば協調ロール、delegation id、契約、段階イベントが必要なら委任と引き継ぎに属します。

semantic_area.zh: 责任委派与智能体工具调用
semantic_area.ja: 責任委任とエージェントツール呼び出し

overlap_reason.zh: 两者都可能请求另一智能体执行工作，但 DelegationProcess 通过契约移交有范围的工作、权限、上下文或责任；AgentAsToolInvocation 由能力调用语义识别并保留调用者控制和答案责任。 本条仅记录 DelegationContract、Handoff；其 canonical owner 是委派与移交（orchestration-delegation-handoff），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此拥有这些概念。
overlap_reason.en: Both may request work from another agent, but DelegationProcess transfers scoped work, authority, context, or responsibility under a contract; AgentAsToolInvocation is identified by capability-call semantics and retains caller control and answer responsibility. This record is limited to DelegationContract, Handoff; their canonical owner is Delegation and handoff (orchestration-delegation-handoff), so orchestration-delegation-handoff owns these concepts here.
overlap_reason.ja: どちらも別エージェントへ作業を求め得ますが、DelegationProcess は契約に基づき範囲付き作業・権限・コンテキスト・責任を移し、AgentAsToolInvocation は能力呼び出し意味で識別され呼び出し元の制御と回答責任を保持します。 この記録は DelegationContract、Handoff だけを対象とし、canonical owner は委任と引き継ぎ（orchestration-delegation-handoff）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを所有します。

disambiguation_test.zh: 若存在委派契约并转交工作范围、权限或责任，归委派与移交；若调用者保留最终答案和会话控制且接收 ToolResult，归调用与执行。
disambiguation_test.en: If a delegation contract transfers work scope, authority, or responsibility, it belongs to delegation and handoff; if the caller retains final-answer and conversation control and receives a ToolResult, it belongs to invocation and execution.
disambiguation_test.ja: 委任契約が作業範囲・権限・責任を移すなら委任と引き継ぎ、呼び出し元が最終回答と会話制御を保持し ToolResult を受け取るなら呼び出しと実行に属します。

semantic_area.zh: 工作者选择与工具选择

overlap_reason.zh: 两者都评估能力与可用性，但选择 Worker 会建立职责、上下文与授权边界；选择 Tool 只选择可调用能力，不转移责任。 本条仅记录 WorkerSelection、WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity；其 canonical owner 是委派与移交（orchestration-delegation-handoff），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此拥有这些概念。
overlap_reason.en: Both assess capability and availability, but selecting a Worker establishes responsibility, context, and authority boundaries; selecting a Tool chooses callable capability without transferring responsibility. This record is limited to WorkerSelection, WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity; their canonical owner is Delegation and handoff (orchestration-delegation-handoff), so orchestration-delegation-handoff owns these concepts here.
overlap_reason.ja: どちらも capability/availability を評価しますが、Worker selection は responsibility・context・authority boundary を確立し、Tool selection は callable capability を選ぶだけで responsibility を移しません。 この記録は WorkerSelection、WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity だけを対象とし、canonical owner は委任と引き継ぎ（orchestration-delegation-handoff）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを所有します。

disambiguation_test.zh: 若结果需要 DelegationContract、WorkerRole 或 ResponsibilityTransfer，归委派与移交；若结果是 ToolCandidate/ToolSelectionDecision，归能力发现。
disambiguation_test.en: If the result requires DelegationContract, WorkerRole, or ResponsibilityTransfer, it belongs to delegation and handoff; if it is ToolCandidate or ToolSelectionDecision, it belongs to capability discovery.
disambiguation_test.ja: 結果に DelegationContract・WorkerRole・ResponsibilityTransfer が必要なら delegation/handoff、ToolCandidate/ToolSelectionDecision なら capability discovery に属します。

semantic_area.zh: 工作者选择与工具选择

overlap_reason.zh: 两者都评估能力与可用性，但选择 Worker 会建立职责、上下文与授权边界；选择 Tool 只选择可调用能力，不转移责任。 本条仅记录 PromptSelectionDecision、SelectionDecision；其 canonical owner 是能力发现与选择（tool-discovery-selection），因此 orchestration-delegation-handoff 在此引用这些概念。
overlap_reason.en: Both assess capability and availability, but selecting a Worker establishes responsibility, context, and authority boundaries; selecting a Tool chooses callable capability without transferring responsibility. This record is limited to PromptSelectionDecision, SelectionDecision; their canonical owner is Capability discovery and selection (tool-discovery-selection), so orchestration-delegation-handoff references these concepts here.
overlap_reason.ja: どちらも capability/availability を評価しますが、Worker selection は responsibility・context・authority boundary を確立し、Tool selection は callable capability を選ぶだけで responsibility を移しません。 この記録は PromptSelectionDecision、SelectionDecision だけを対象とし、canonical owner は能力発見と選択（tool-discovery-selection）です。したがって orchestration-delegation-handoff はここでこれらを参照します。

short_definitions.zh: 接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任。
short_definitions.en: As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility.
short_definitions.ja: 委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です。

definitions.zh: 接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任。
definitions.en: As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility.
definitions.ja: 委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です。

why_needed.zh: 接受阶段为canonical 事实 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase（AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase）提供明确端点并保持与 CompletionPhase 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: Acceptance phase provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase (AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase) while preserving its reviewed boundary from CompletionPhase.
why_needed.ja: 受諾段階はcanonical 事実 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase（AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase）の明確な端点を提供し、CompletionPhase との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

includes.zh: 纳入符合下列已审查种差的接受阶段：接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任。；该种差可由关系 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase 的端点角色检验。
includes.en: Includes Acceptance phase records satisfying this reviewed differentia: As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility. The differentia can be checked through its endpoint role in AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase.
includes.ja: 次の審査済み種差を満たす受諾段階を含みます：委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です。 種差は関係 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase の端点役割で検査できます。

excludes.zh: 完成阶段虽与本概念同属 DelegationPhase，但其定义为“完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭。”，不得替代接受阶段。
excludes.en: CompletionPhase shares parent DelegationPhase, but its reviewed definition is “Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract.”; it must not be substituted for AcceptancePhase.
excludes.ja: CompletionPhase は同じ上位 DelegationPhase を持ちますが、審査済み定義は「完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です。」であり、AcceptancePhase の代用にはできません。

differentia.zh: 端点特定对照：父节点 DelegationPhase：AcceptancePhase（semantic kind=state，骨架 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）；CompletionPhase（semantic kind=state，骨架 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）。双端条件：AcceptancePhase 条件：“接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任”；CompletionPhase 条件：“完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭”。条件不得互借；各自成立后方可归类。
differentia.en: Endpoint-specific contrast: parent DelegationPhase: AcceptancePhase (semantic kind=state, backbone AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase); CompletionPhase (semantic kind=state, backbone CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase). Endpoint conditions: AcceptancePhase condition: “As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility”; CompletionPhase condition: “Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract”. Conditions cannot be borrowed; classify each endpoint only from its own evidence.
differentia.ja: 端点別対照：親 DelegationPhase：AcceptancePhase（semantic kind=state、バックボーン AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）；CompletionPhase（semantic kind=state、バックボーン CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）。両端条件：AcceptancePhase の条件：「委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です」；CompletionPhase の条件：「完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です」。条件を流用せず、各端を固有の証拠で分類します。

descriptions.zh: 在一次协调器的任务分解与委派运行中，系统将接受阶段（AcceptancePhase）记录为 AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase 的源端点。其已审查定义是“接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任”。
descriptions.en: During a coordinator run for task decomposition and delegation, the system records Acceptance phase (AcceptancePhase) as the source of AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase. Its reviewed definition is “As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility”.
descriptions.ja: コーディネーターによるタスク分解と委任の実行では、システムは受諾段階（AcceptancePhase）を AcceptancePhase is_a DelegationPhase の始点として記録します。審査済み定義は「委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です」です。

expected_result.zh: 该运行可查询 AcceptancePhase 以及方向保持不变的事实 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase。
expected_result.en: The run can query AcceptancePhase together with direction-preserving fact AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase.
expected_result.ja: この実行では AcceptancePhase と方向を保持した事実 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase を共に照会できます。

descriptions.zh: CompletionPhase 用作AcceptancePhase的边界对照：两者可见于同一图谱片段，但 CompletionPhase 的已审查定义是“完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭。”。不能因 shared parent DelegationPhase 而把它归入AcceptancePhase。
descriptions.en: CompletionPhase is the boundary contrast for AcceptancePhase. Both may occur in one graph fragment, but CompletionPhase's reviewed definition is “Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract.”. shared parent DelegationPhase does not make it a AcceptancePhase.
descriptions.ja: CompletionPhase は AcceptancePhase の境界対照です。同じグラフ断片に現れ得ますが、CompletionPhase の審査済み定義は「完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です。」です。shared parent DelegationPhase だけで AcceptancePhase にはなりません。

decision_note.zh: 领域审查确认 AcceptancePhase（接受阶段）由 orchestration-delegation-handoff 拥有，用于“当对象的身份由一次工作者选择、委派契约、发起接受撤销完成阶段，或控制和责任的转移决定时，在此拥有。”；领域审查不决定 semantic kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Domain review confirms AcceptancePhase (Acceptance phase) is owned by orchestration-delegation-handoff for the use case “Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.”; domain review does not decide semantic kind or parent placement.
decision_note.ja: ドメイン審査は AcceptancePhase（受諾段階）を orchestration-delegation-handoff が「対象の同一性が一回の作業者選択、委任契約、開始・受諾・取消・完了段階、または制御・責任移転で決まる場合に所有します。」用途で所有することを確認し、semantic kind や親配置は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: 本体审查确认 AcceptancePhase 的 semantic kind 为 state，主父或骨架为 AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase，并满足“semantic kind=state；backbone=AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase；canonical ID 与唯一 owner 保持稳定”；本体审查不决定领域用例。
decision_note.en: Ontology review confirms AcceptancePhase has semantic kind state, primary parent or backbone AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase, and invariants “semantic kind=state; backbone=AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase; canonical ID and unique owner remain stable”; ontology review does not decide the domain use case.
decision_note.ja: 本体審査は AcceptancePhase の semantic kind が state、主親または骨格が AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase であり、「semantic kind=state、backbone=AcceptancePhase-is_a-DelegationPhase、canonical ID と一意の owner を維持」を満たすことを確認し、ドメイン用途は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: AcceptancePhase 的中英日术语、标签与定义表达已分别复核，语言审查不决定 owner、kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Terminology, labels, and definition wording for AcceptancePhase were reviewed across three languages; language review does not decide owner, kind, or parent placement.

short_definitions.zh: 协作过程是两个或更多参与者围绕共同工作目标协调责任、控制权或产出的活动。
short_definitions.en: A collaboration process is an activity in which two or more participants coordinate responsibility, control, or deliverables around a shared work objective.
short_definitions.ja: 活動に属する協働プロセスは、次の事実を種差とします：それは、二者以上の参加者が共通の作業目標をめぐって責任、制御、成果物を調整する活動です。

definitions.zh: 协作过程是两个或更多参与者围绕共同工作目标协调责任、控制权或产出的活动。
definitions.en: A collaboration process is an activity in which two or more participants coordinate responsibility, control, or deliverables around a shared work objective.
definitions.ja: 活動に属する協働プロセスは、次の事実を種差とします：それは、二者以上の参加者が共通の作業目標をめぐって責任、制御、成果物を調整する活動です。

why_needed.zh: 协作过程为canonical 事实 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess（DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess）提供明确端点并保持与 DelegationProcess 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: Collaboration process provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess (DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DelegationProcess.
why_needed.ja: 協働プロセスはcanonical 事実 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess（DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess）の明確な端点を提供し、DelegationProcess との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

includes.zh: 纳入符合下列已审查种差的协作过程：协作过程是两个或更多参与者围绕共同工作目标协调责任、控制权或产出的活动。；该种差可由关系 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess 的端点角色检验。
includes.en: Includes Collaboration process records satisfying this reviewed differentia: A collaboration process is an activity in which two or more participants coordinate responsibility, control, or deliverables around a shared work objective. The differentia can be checked through its endpoint role in DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess.
includes.ja: 次の審査済み種差を満たす協働プロセスを含みます：活動に属する協働プロセスは、次の事実を種差とします：それは、二者以上の参加者が共通の作業目標をめぐって責任、制御、成果物を調整する活動です。 種差は関係 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess の端点役割で検査できます。

excludes.zh: 委派过程只是关系 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess 的另一端；相关联不表示它是协作过程。
excludes.en: DelegationProcess is only the other endpoint of DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess; relatedness does not make it a CollaborationProcess.
excludes.ja: DelegationProcess は DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess のもう一方の端点にすぎず、関連するだけで CollaborationProcess にはなりません。

descriptions.zh: 在一次协调器的任务分解与委派运行中，系统将协作过程（CollaborationProcess）记录为 DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess 的目标端点。其已审查定义是“协作过程是两个或更多参与者围绕共同工作目标协调责任、控制权或产出的活动”。
descriptions.en: During a coordinator run for task decomposition and delegation, the system records Collaboration process (CollaborationProcess) as the target of DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess. Its reviewed definition is “A collaboration process is an activity in which two or more participants coordinate responsibility, control, or deliverables around a shared work objective”.
descriptions.ja: コーディネーターによるタスク分解と委任の実行では、システムは協働プロセス（CollaborationProcess）を DelegationProcess is_a CollaborationProcess の終点として記録します。審査済み定義は「活動に属する協働プロセスは、次の事実を種差とします：それは、二者以上の参加者が共通の作業目標をめぐって責任、制御、成果物を調整する活動です」です。

expected_result.zh: 该运行可查询 CollaborationProcess 以及方向保持不变的事实 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess。
expected_result.en: The run can query CollaborationProcess together with direction-preserving fact DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess.
expected_result.ja: この実行では CollaborationProcess と方向を保持した事実 DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess を共に照会できます。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 该场景同时给出真实运行语境、DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess 的 canonical 方向、概念定义和可用字段值。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The scenario combines a real operational context, the canonical direction of DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess, the concept definition, and available field values.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: このシナリオは実際の運用文脈、DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess の canonical 方向、概念定義、利用可能な項目値を組み合わせます。

labels.ja: 協働プロセスの境界例

descriptions.zh: DelegationProcess 用作CollaborationProcess的边界对照：两者可见于同一图谱片段，但 DelegationProcess 的已审查定义是“委派过程是以如下事实为区分特征的协作过程：它是委派者依据合同把有范围的工作、权限、上下文与预算交给代理角色执行，并接收可追踪结果而保持或转移明确归属的协调活动。”。不能因 relation DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess 而把它归入CollaborationProcess。
descriptions.en: DelegationProcess is the boundary contrast for CollaborationProcess. Both may occur in one graph fragment, but DelegationProcess's reviewed definition is “Delegation process is a Collaboration process; its differentiating condition is that it is a coordination activity in which a delegator assigns scoped work, authority, context, and budget under a contract to a delegate role and receives a traceable result with explicit retained or transferred ownership.”. relation DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess does not make it a CollaborationProcess.
descriptions.ja: DelegationProcess は CollaborationProcess の境界対照です。同じグラフ断片に現れ得ますが、DelegationProcess の審査済み定義は「委任プロセスは協働プロセスの一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：DelegationProcess は委任者が契約に基づき範囲付き作業、権限、文脈、予算を代理役割へ割り当て、明示された保持・移転帰属と共に追跡可能な結果を受け取る調整活動である。」です。relation DelegationProcess-is_a-CollaborationProcess だけで CollaborationProcess にはなりません。

expected_result.zh: DelegationProcess 保持其原有类型，验证器不得凭关系共现把它提升为 CollaborationProcess。
expected_result.en: DelegationProcess retains its own type; validation must not promote it to CollaborationProcess from relation co-occurrence.
expected_result.ja: DelegationProcess は元の型を維持し、関係の共起だけで CollaborationProcess へ昇格してはなりません。

short_definitions.zh: 完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭。
short_definitions.en: Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract.
short_definitions.ja: 完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です。

definitions.zh: 完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭。
definitions.en: Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract.
definitions.ja: 完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です。

why_needed.zh: 完成阶段为canonical 事实 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase（CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase）提供明确端点并保持与 AcceptancePhase 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: Completion phase provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase (CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase) while preserving its reviewed boundary from AcceptancePhase.
why_needed.ja: 完了段階はcanonical 事実 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase（CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase）の明確な端点を提供し、AcceptancePhase との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

includes.zh: 纳入符合下列已审查种差的完成阶段：完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭。；该种差可由关系 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase 的端点角色检验。
includes.en: Includes Completion phase records satisfying this reviewed differentia: Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract. The differentia can be checked through its endpoint role in CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase.
includes.ja: 次の審査済み種差を満たす完了段階を含みます：完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です。 種差は関係 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase の端点役割で検査できます。

excludes.zh: 接受阶段虽与本概念同属 DelegationPhase，但其定义为“接受阶段是一种委派阶段；其区分条件是：受派方明确接受委派合同并承担约定责任。”，不得替代完成阶段。
excludes.en: AcceptancePhase shares parent DelegationPhase, but its reviewed definition is “As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility.”; it must not be substituted for CompletionPhase.
excludes.ja: AcceptancePhase は同じ上位 DelegationPhase を持ちますが、審査済み定義は「委任段階に属する受諾段階は、次の事実を種差とします：受任者が委任契約を明示的に受諾し、合意した責任を引き受ける段階です。」であり、CompletionPhase の代用にはできません。

differentia.zh: 共享父节点对照：父节点 DelegationPhase：CompletionPhase（semantic kind=state，骨架 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）；InitiationPhase（semantic kind=state，骨架 InitiationPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase）。双端条件：CompletionPhase 条件：“完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭”；InitiationPhase 条件：“作为委派阶段，发起阶段由这一事实区分：委派方提出工作范围、权限与验收条件，受派方尚未接受”。逐端验证；父节点不证明任一身份。
differentia.en: Shared-parent contrast: parent DelegationPhase: CompletionPhase (semantic kind=state, backbone CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase); InitiationPhase (semantic kind=state, backbone InitiationPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase). Endpoint conditions: CompletionPhase condition: “Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract”; InitiationPhase condition: “Initiation phase belongs to the Delegation phase category and differs from broader members in that the delegator proposes scope, authority, and acceptance conditions before the delegate accepts”. Verify each endpoint; the parent proves neither identity.

descriptions.zh: 在一次协调器的任务分解与委派运行中，系统将完成阶段（CompletionPhase）记录为 CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase 的源端点。其已审查定义是“完成阶段是以如下事实为区分特征的委派阶段：受派方返回约定结果与证据，委派责任按照合同完成或关闭”。
descriptions.en: During a coordinator run for task decomposition and delegation, the system records Completion phase (CompletionPhase) as the source of CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase. Its reviewed definition is “Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract”.
descriptions.ja: コーディネーターによるタスク分解と委任の実行では、システムは完了段階（CompletionPhase）を CompletionPhase is_a DelegationPhase の始点として記録します。審査済み定義は「完了段階は委任段階の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：受任者が合意した結果と証拠を返し、契約に基づく委任責任を完了または終了する段階です」です。

expected_result.zh: 该运行可查询 CompletionPhase 以及方向保持不变的事实 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase。
expected_result.en: The run can query CompletionPhase together with direction-preserving fact CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase.
expected_result.ja: この実行では CompletionPhase と方向を保持した事実 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase を共に照会できます。

decision_note.zh: 领域审查确认 CompletionPhase（完成阶段）由 orchestration-delegation-handoff 拥有，用于“当对象的身份由一次工作者选择、委派契约、发起接受撤销完成阶段，或控制和责任的转移决定时，在此拥有。”；领域审查不决定 semantic kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Domain review confirms CompletionPhase (Completion phase) is owned by orchestration-delegation-handoff for the use case “Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.”; domain review does not decide semantic kind or parent placement.
decision_note.ja: ドメイン審査は CompletionPhase（完了段階）を orchestration-delegation-handoff が「対象の同一性が一回の作業者選択、委任契約、開始・受諾・取消・完了段階、または制御・責任移転で決まる場合に所有します。」用途で所有することを確認し、semantic kind や親配置は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: 本体审查确认 CompletionPhase 的 semantic kind 为 state，主父或骨架为 CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase，并满足“semantic kind=state；backbone=CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase；canonical ID 与唯一 owner 保持稳定”；本体审查不决定领域用例。
decision_note.en: Ontology review confirms CompletionPhase has semantic kind state, primary parent or backbone CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase, and invariants “semantic kind=state; backbone=CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase; canonical ID and unique owner remain stable”; ontology review does not decide the domain use case.
decision_note.ja: 本体審査は CompletionPhase の semantic kind が state、主親または骨格が CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase であり、「semantic kind=state、backbone=CompletionPhase-is_a-DelegationPhase、canonical ID と一意の owner を維持」を満たすことを確認し、ドメイン用途は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: CompletionPhase 的中英日术语、标签与定义表达已分别复核，语言审查不决定 owner、kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Terminology, labels, and definition wording for CompletionPhase were reviewed across three languages; language review does not decide owner, kind, or parent placement.
decision_note.ja: CompletionPhase の中英日三言語の用語・ラベル・定義表現を個別に審査し、言語審査は owner・kind・親配置を決定しません。

change_note.ja: 完了段階は明示設定に基づき semantic kind、所有者、ルート状態、レイアウト親を再審査しました。

labels.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様

short_definitions.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范是以如下事实为区分特征的规范：它是为一次委派明确可见与排除的消息、记忆、工具和来源的边界规范；它不表示安全沙箱隔离，也不表示被传递的上下文内容本身。
short_definitions.en: Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself.
short_definitions.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません。

definitions.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范是以如下事实为区分特征的规范：它是为一次委派明确可见与排除的消息、记忆、工具和来源的边界规范；它不表示安全沙箱隔离，也不表示被传递的上下文内容本身。
definitions.en: Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself.
definitions.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません。

why_needed.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范为canonical 事实 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext（ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext）提供明确端点并保持与 DelegationContext 的定义边界；节点字段 isolation_spec_id、version、allowed_surface_refs 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: Delegation context-isolation specification provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext (ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DelegationContext; node fields isolation_spec_id, version, allowed_surface_refs make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様はcanonical 事実 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext（ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext）の明確な端点を提供し、DelegationContext との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 isolation_spec_id、version、allowed_surface_refs によりその区別を検証できます。

includes.zh: 纳入符合下列已审查种差的委派上下文隔离规范：委派上下文隔离规范是以如下事实为区分特征的规范：它是为一次委派明确可见与排除的消息、记忆、工具和来源的边界规范；它不表示安全沙箱隔离，也不表示被传递的上下文内容本身。；该种差可由字段 isolation_spec_id、version、allowed_surface_refs 检验。
includes.en: Includes Delegation context-isolation specification records satisfying this reviewed differentia: Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself. The differentia can be checked through fields isolation_spec_id, version, allowed_surface_refs.
includes.ja: 次の審査済み種差を満たす委任コンテキスト分離仕様を含みます：委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません。 種差は項目 isolation_spec_id、version、allowed_surface_refs で検査できます。

excludes.zh: 委派上下文只是关系 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext 的另一端；相关联不表示它是委派上下文隔离规范。
excludes.en: DelegationContext is only the other endpoint of ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext; relatedness does not make it a ContextIsolation.
excludes.ja: DelegationContext は ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext のもう一方の端点にすぎず、関連するだけで ContextIsolation にはなりません。

differentia.zh: 正向身份比较：父节点 DelegationContract：ContextIsolation（semantic kind=specification，骨架 DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation）；DelegatedAuthority（semantic kind=specification，骨架 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority）。双端条件：ContextIsolation 条件：“委派上下文隔离规范是以如下事实为区分特征的规范：它是为一次委派明确可见与排除的消息、记忆、工具和来源的边界规范；它不表示安全沙箱隔离，也不表示被传递的上下文内容本身”；DelegatedAuthority 条件：“受委派权限是一种规范；其区分条件是：它说明受托方可代表编排者使用的权限、工具、记忆表面、数据区域和操作”。仅依据各自定义与骨架逐端验证。
differentia.en: Positive identity comparison: parent DelegationContract: ContextIsolation (semantic kind=specification, backbone DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation); DelegatedAuthority (semantic kind=specification, backbone DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority). Endpoint conditions: ContextIsolation condition: “Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself”; DelegatedAuthority condition: “As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator”. Verify each endpoint from its own definition and backbone.
differentia.ja: 正の同一性比較：親 DelegationContract：ContextIsolation（semantic kind=specification、バックボーン DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation）；DelegatedAuthority（semantic kind=specification、バックボーン DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority）。両端条件：ContextIsolation の条件：「委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません」；DelegatedAuthority の条件：「仕様に属する委任権限は、次の事実を種差とします：それは、受任者がオーケストレータに代わって使える権限、道具、記憶面、データ領域、操作を示します」。各端を固有の定義とバックボーンで検証します。

differentia.en: Endpoint-specific contrast: parent DelegationContract: ContextIsolation (semantic kind=specification, backbone DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation); DelegationBudget (semantic kind=specification, backbone constrained_by_budget). Conditions cannot be borrowed; classify each endpoint only from its own evidence.
differentia.ja: 端点別対照：親 DelegationContract：ContextIsolation（semantic kind=specification、バックボーン DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation）；DelegationBudget（semantic kind=specification、バックボーン constrained_by_budget）。両端条件：ContextIsolation の条件：「委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません」；DelegationBudget の条件：「委任予算は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、トークン、時間、費用、再試行、道具呼び出し、文脈の上限を設定し、委任作業と経路選択を制約します」。条件を流用せず、各端を固有の証拠で分類します。

labels.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范正例
labels.en: Positive example of Delegation context-isolation specification
labels.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様の正例

descriptions.zh: 在一次协调器的任务分解与委派运行中，系统将委派上下文隔离规范（ContextIsolation）记录为 ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext 的源端点。其已审查定义是“委派上下文隔离规范是以如下事实为区分特征的规范：它是为一次委派明确可见与排除的消息、记忆、工具和来源的边界规范；它不表示安全沙箱隔离，也不表示被传递的上下文内容本身”。记录同时写入经审查字段 isolation_spec_id="context-isolation-001", version="1", allowed_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:task"], denied_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:private"], default_decision="deny", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z"。
descriptions.en: During a coordinator run for task decomposition and delegation, the system records Delegation context-isolation specification (ContextIsolation) as the source of ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext. Its reviewed definition is “Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself”. The record also binds reviewed fields isolation_spec_id="context-isolation-001", version="1", allowed_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:task"], denied_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:private"], default_decision="deny", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z".
descriptions.ja: コーディネーターによるタスク分解と委任の実行では、システムは委任コンテキスト分離仕様（ContextIsolation）を ContextIsolation bounds DelegationContext の始点として記録します。審査済み定義は「委任コンテキスト分離仕様は仕様の一種であり、次の条件で区別されます：それは、一回の委任について可視または除外するメッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を宣言する境界仕様であり、安全サンドボックス分離でも委任されるコンテキスト内容そのものでもありません」です。記録には審査済み項目 isolation_spec_id="context-isolation-001", version="1", allowed_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:task"], denied_surface_refs=["MemorySurface:private"], default_decision="deny", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z" も設定されます。

expected_result.zh: 该运行可查询 ContextIsolation 以及方向保持不变的事实 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext。
expected_result.en: The run can query ContextIsolation together with direction-preserving fact ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext.
expected_result.ja: この実行では ContextIsolation と方向を保持した事実 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext を共に照会できます。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 该场景同时给出真实运行语境、ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext 的 canonical 方向、概念定义和可用字段值。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The scenario combines a real operational context, the canonical direction of ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext, the concept definition, and available field values.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: このシナリオは実際の運用文脈、ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext の canonical 方向、概念定義、利用可能な項目値を組み合わせます。

labels.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范边界例
labels.en: Boundary case for Delegation context-isolation specification
labels.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様の境界例

descriptions.zh: DelegationContext 用作ContextIsolation的边界对照：两者可见于同一图谱片段，但 DelegationContext 的已审查定义是“作为信息记录，委派上下文由这一事实区分：它是按一项委派契约和上下文隔离规范形成的有界上下文信息，记录受派工作所需且获准披露的任务状态、消息、记忆、工具和来源。”。不能因 relation ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext 而把它归入ContextIsolation。
descriptions.en: DelegationContext is the boundary contrast for ContextIsolation. Both may occur in one graph fragment, but DelegationContext's reviewed definition is “Delegation context belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is bounded context information formed under one delegation contract and context-isolation specification, recording the task state, messages, memory, tools, and sources required and authorized for delegated work.”. relation ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext does not make it a ContextIsolation.
descriptions.ja: DelegationContext は ContextIsolation の境界対照です。同じグラフ断片に現れ得ますが、DelegationContext の審査済み定義は「委任コンテキストは情報記録として、次の条件により他の対象と区別されます：それは、一つの委任契約とコンテキスト分離仕様の下で形成され、委任作業に必要かつ開示を許可されたタスク状態、メッセージ、記憶、ツール、情報源を記録する境界付きコンテキスト情報です。」です。relation ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext だけで ContextIsolation にはなりません。

expected_result.zh: DelegationContext 保持其原有类型，验证器不得凭关系共现把它提升为 ContextIsolation。
expected_result.en: DelegationContext retains its own type; validation must not promote it to ContextIsolation from relation co-occurrence.
expected_result.ja: DelegationContext は元の型を維持し、関係の共起だけで ContextIsolation へ昇格してはなりません。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 边界例点名实际对照节点，并保留 ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext 的方向；只有显式 is_a 或完整种差才支持成员资格。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The boundary names an actual contrast node and preserves the direction of ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext; only an explicit is_a fact or the complete differentia supports membership.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 境界例は実在する対照ノードを示し、ContextIsolation-bounds-DelegationContext の方向を保持します。明示的 is_a または完全な種差だけが所属を支持します。

decision_note.zh: 领域审查确认 ContextIsolation（委派上下文隔离规范）由 orchestration-delegation-handoff 拥有，用于“当对象的身份由一次工作者选择、委派契约、发起接受撤销完成阶段，或控制和责任的转移决定时，在此拥有。”；领域审查不决定 semantic kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Domain review confirms ContextIsolation (Delegation context-isolation specification) is owned by orchestration-delegation-handoff for the use case “Own an object when its identity is determined by one worker selection, delegation contract, initiation/acceptance/revocation/completion phase, or transfer of control or responsibility.”; domain review does not decide semantic kind or parent placement.
decision_note.ja: ドメイン審査は ContextIsolation（委任コンテキスト分離仕様）を orchestration-delegation-handoff が「対象の同一性が一回の作業者選択、委任契約、開始・受諾・取消・完了段階、または制御・責任移転で決まる場合に所有します。」用途で所有することを確認し、semantic kind や親配置は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: 本体审查确认 ContextIsolation 的 semantic kind 为 specification，主父或骨架为 DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation，并满足“semantic kind=specification；backbone=DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation；canonical ID 与唯一 owner 保持稳定”；本体审查不决定领域用例。
decision_note.en: Ontology review confirms ContextIsolation has semantic kind specification, primary parent or backbone DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation, and invariants “semantic kind=specification; backbone=DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation; canonical ID and unique owner remain stable”; ontology review does not decide the domain use case.
decision_note.ja: 本体審査は ContextIsolation の semantic kind が specification、主親または骨格が DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation であり、「semantic kind=specification、backbone=DelegationContract-specifies-ContextIsolation、canonical ID と一意の owner を維持」を満たすことを確認し、ドメイン用途は決定しません。

decision_note.zh: ContextIsolation 的中英日术语、标签与定义表达已分别复核，语言审查不决定 owner、kind 或父关系。
decision_note.en: Terminology, labels, and definition wording for ContextIsolation were reviewed across three languages; language review does not decide owner, kind, or parent placement.
decision_note.ja: ContextIsolation の中英日三言語の用語・ラベル・定義表現を個別に審査し、言語審査は owner・kind・親配置を決定しません。

change_note.zh: 委派上下文隔离规范已按显式配置复核 semantic kind、owner、根状态与布局父关系。
change_note.en: Delegation context-isolation specification was reviewed against the explicit semantic-kind, ownership, root-status, and layout-parent decisions.
change_note.ja: 委任コンテキスト分離仕様は明示設定に基づき semantic kind、所有者、ルート状態、レイアウト親を再審査しました。

short_definitions.zh: 受委派权限是一种规范；其区分条件是：它说明受托方可代表编排者使用的权限、工具、记忆表面、数据区域和操作。
short_definitions.en: As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator.
short_definitions.ja: 仕様に属する委任権限は、次の事実を種差とします：それは、受任者がオーケストレータに代わって使える権限、道具、記憶面、データ領域、操作を示します。

definitions.zh: 受委派权限是一种规范；其区分条件是：它说明受托方可代表编排者使用的权限、工具、记忆表面、数据区域和操作。
definitions.en: As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator.
definitions.ja: 仕様に属する委任権限は、次の事実を種差とします：それは、受任者がオーケストレータに代わって使える権限、道具、記憶面、データ領域、操作を示します。

why_needed.zh: 受委派权限为canonical 事实 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority（DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority）提供明确端点并保持与 DelegationContract 的定义边界；节点字段 authority_id、version、delegatee_id 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: delegated authority provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority (DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DelegationContract; node fields authority_id, version, delegatee_id make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 委任権限はcanonical 事実 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority（DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority）の明確な端点を提供し、DelegationContract との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 authority_id、version、delegatee_id によりその区別を検証できます。

includes.zh: 纳入符合下列已审查种差的受委派权限：受委派权限是一种规范；其区分条件是：它说明受托方可代表编排者使用的权限、工具、记忆表面、数据区域和操作。；该种差可由字段 authority_id、version、delegatee_id 检验。
includes.en: Includes delegated authority records satisfying this reviewed differentia: As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator. The differentia can be checked through fields authority_id, version, delegatee_id.
includes.ja: 次の審査済み種差を満たす委任権限を含みます：仕様に属する委任権限は、次の事実を種差とします：それは、受任者がオーケストレータに代わって使える権限、道具、記憶面、データ領域、操作を示します。 種差は項目 authority_id、version、delegatee_id で検査できます。

excludes.zh: 委派契约只是关系 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority 的另一端；相关联不表示它是受委派权限。
excludes.en: DelegationContract is only the other endpoint of DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority; relatedness does not make it a DelegatedAuthority.
excludes.ja: DelegationContract は DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority のもう一方の端点にすぎず、関連するだけで DelegatedAuthority にはなりません。

descriptions.zh: 在一次协调器的任务分解与委派运行中，系统将受委派权限（DelegatedAuthority）记录为 DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority 的目标端点。其已审查定义是“受委派权限是一种规范；其区分条件是：它说明受托方可代表编排者使用的权限、工具、记忆表面、数据区域和操作”。记录同时写入经审查字段 authority_id="authority-delegation-001", version="1", delegatee_id="Actor:worker-007", permission_refs=["Permission:read-repo"], resource_scope_refs=["Repository:moonweave"], approved_by="Actor:orchestrator-001", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z", valid_until="2026-07-15T03:00:00Z"。
descriptions.en: During a coordinator run for task decomposition and delegation, the system records delegated authority (DelegatedAuthority) as the target of DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority. Its reviewed definition is “As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator”. The record also binds reviewed fields authority_id="authority-delegation-001", version="1", delegatee_id="Actor:worker-007", permission_refs=["Permission:read-repo"], resource_scope_refs=["Repository:moonweave"], approved_by="Actor:orchestrator-001", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z", valid_until="2026-07-15T03:00:00Z".
descriptions.ja: コーディネーターによるタスク分解と委任の実行では、システムは委任権限（DelegatedAuthority）を DelegationContract grants DelegatedAuthority の終点として記録します。審査済み定義は「仕様に属する委任権限は、次の事実を種差とします：それは、受任者がオーケストレータに代わって使える権限、道具、記憶面、データ領域、操作を示します」です。記録には審査済み項目 authority_id="authority-delegation-001", version="1", delegatee_id="Actor:worker-007", permission_refs=["Permission:read-repo"], resource_scope_refs=["Repository:moonweave"], approved_by="Actor:orchestrator-001", valid_from="2026-07-15T01:00:00Z", valid_until="2026-07-15T03:00:00Z" も設定されます。

expected_result.zh: 该运行可查询 DelegatedAuthority 以及方向保持不变的事实 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority。
expected_result.en: The run can query DelegatedAuthority together with direction-preserving fact DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority.
expected_result.ja: この実行では DelegatedAuthority と方向を保持した事実 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority を共に照会できます。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 该场景同时给出真实运行语境、DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority 的 canonical 方向、概念定义和可用字段值。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The scenario combines a real operational context, the canonical direction of DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority, the concept definition, and available field values.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: このシナリオは実際の運用文脈、DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority の canonical 方向、概念定義、利用可能な項目値を組み合わせます。

descriptions.zh: DelegationContract 用作DelegatedAuthority的边界对照：两者可见于同一图谱片段，但 DelegationContract 的已审查定义是“委派契约属于规范，区别于其他同类对象的条件是：它规定委派工作、预期结果、授权范围、可见上下文、预算、完成准则以及委派方和受托方的归属边界。”。不能因 relation DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority 而把它归入DelegatedAuthority。
descriptions.en: DelegationContract is the boundary contrast for DelegatedAuthority. Both may occur in one graph fragment, but DelegationContract's reviewed definition is “Delegation contract is a specification whose defining condition is that it specifies delegated work, expected result, authority scope, visible context, budget, completion criteria, and ownership boundaries between delegator and delegatee.”. relation DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority does not make it a DelegatedAuthority.
descriptions.ja: DelegationContract は DelegatedAuthority の境界対照です。同じグラフ断片に現れ得ますが、DelegationContract の審査済み定義は「委任契約は仕様のうち、次の事実を定義条件とする対象です：それは、委任作業、期待される結果、権限範囲、可視文脈、予算、完了基準、委任者と受任者の帰属境界を定めます。」です。relation DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority だけで DelegatedAuthority にはなりません。

expected_result.zh: DelegationContract 保持其原有类型，验证器不得凭关系共现把它提升为 DelegatedAuthority。
expected_result.en: DelegationContract retains its own type; validation must not promote it to DelegatedAuthority from relation co-occurrence.
expected_result.ja: DelegationContract は元の型を維持し、関係の共起だけで DelegatedAuthority へ昇格してはなりません。

why_valid_or_invalid.zh: 边界例点名实际对照节点，并保留 DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority 的方向；只有显式 is_a 或完整种差才支持成员资格。
why_valid_or_invalid.en: The boundary names an actual contrast node and preserves the direction of DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority; only an explicit is_a fact or the complete differentia supports membership.
why_valid_or_invalid.ja: 境界例は実在する対照ノードを示し、DelegationContract-grants-DelegatedAuthority の方向を保持します。明示的 is_a または完全な種差だけが所属を支持します。
